articles.full_text:
Several areas to hit ‘danger’ level heat indices on Sunday, May 10
MANILA, Philippines — Several areas across the country are forecast to experience “danger” level heat indices on Sunday – Mother’s Day – May 10, the state weather bureau said.
According to the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa), heat index pertains to the “apparent heat” or how hot the body feels relative to the air temperature and humidity.
For Sunday, heat indices ranging from 42 to 44 °C are expected in 26 stations nationwide.
In Metro Manila, heat indices may reach up to 42°C in Pasay City and 41°C in Quezon City.
Under Pagasa’s classification, a heat index between 42°C and 51°C falls under the “danger” category, where heat cramps and heat exhaustion are likely.
Heat stroke also becomes possible with prolonged exposure or following strenuous activities.
The following areas are forecast to reach danger level heat indices on Sunday:
From March to June, Pagasa regularly releases heat index monitoring to guide the public during the summer. /apl
